text:
We introduce Video-LLaMA, a multi-modal framework aiming to bridge the gap between Large Language Models (LLMs) and video understanding while leveraging both visual and audio information. While many works have demonstrated the feasibility of incorporating either images or speech into LLMs, there has been a lack of research focusing on what we consider the most representative and challenging scenario, i.e., video understanding, which demands the analysis of visual temporal evolution and requires the synchronization of both audio and visual streams.

In this work, we propose a modular multimodal model that leverages both vision and audio modalities in conversational AI, achieving the “voice assistance of the future”. Our model, Video-LLaMA, is comprised of two special designed blocks named Vision-Language Branch and Audio-Language Branch. In particular, the vision branch makes use of the Video Q-former to capture temporal information while connecting with image pre-trained encoders, and the audio branch adopts ImageBind to transform the audio signals into a fixed-size embedding space with the aid of the Audio Q-former. A multi-stage and multi-branch cross-modal pre-training strategy is designed: the first stage and middle stage are pre-trained using the aligning knowledge from large-scale video and image-caption data, while the second stage is fine-tuned on high-quality instruction-following data to support outputting the final answers. Experimental results show that our proposed Video-LLaMA model has learned to not only “see” and “understand” videos but also “speak” ground adequate outputs for users with the help of multi-modal information. We also open-sourced our codebase and the released model weights for the community to advance research in the audio-visual conversational AI assistants.
